Masija, Charlotte's first Korean-style "sojubang" cocktail bar, announced on social media that it will close permanently on March 26, 2026, after just three months of operation since opening in December 2025. The team cited no official reason for the closure but assured fans the concept would return in some form.

The menu features freshly made mini donuts in 20+ flavors, gourmet coffee, premium ice cream, milkshakes, and shareable creations such as donut ice cream sundaes. A July or August 2026 debut is targeted, pending construction and permitting.

short_description: Everybody Loves Raymond's, a family-owned San Antonio burger restaurant, abruptly closed its Downtown location at 2118 I-35 Frontage Rd on March 23, 2026, confirmed via a social media post on March 25. No reason was cited for the closure, and the brand's second location at 18600 Pleasanton Road continues to operate.

Oso Felipe, a Mexican restaurant at 2 Erie Street in Montclair, announced via social media that it will close permanently on March 28, 2026, after more than a year in operation. The restaurant offered tacos, enchiladas, tamales, pozole, seafood, grilled items, and a full drinks menu, occupying a multi-story space with two bars and outdoor seating. No official reason for the closure was stated.

location: 7175 W Lake Mead Blvd, Suite 168, Las Vegas, NV 89128

The Las Vegas opening is led by franchisee and Operations Advisor Gene Zhou as part of the brand's eastward expansion. No official opening date has been announced.

status: "tasting room…set to close to the public after March 28, 2026"
short_description: Barrique Brewing and Blending announced via Facebook that its tasting room at 30 Oldham Street will close on March 28, 2026, with owner Joel Stickrod citing the building's expected removal in 2026 as the reason. The Nashville brewery, founded in 2017, specializes in barrel-aged wild ales and crisp Czech-style lagers. New production was paused after December as part of the wind-down.

The bar's décor features vintage Palm Beach hues of pinks and greens, with cocktails named after the owner's friends and family.

location: Naperville, IL — shopping plaza (exact address not specified)

The brand offers specialty drinks including phin drip coffees, robusta espressos, teas, and egg coffee, as well as merchandise and roasts. No exact address or opening date has been released.

location: 255 E Basse Rd, San Antonio, TX 78209
short_description: Green Vegetarian Cuisine at Alamo Quarry Market is closing after 19 years of operation, with owner Chef Mike Behrend citing rising costs and a change in consumer behavior as reasons for the closure. The restaurant will continue to serve customers through April 17, 2026, operating during regular hours from 10 a.m. to 9 p.m.

status: "planning to open a location in Phoenix" / "An opening date has not been announced"
short_description: The Shop Beer Co., a Tempe-based microbrewery and taproom, is planning to open a 15,000 square-foot Phoenix location at 3002 E. Washington Street, per a liquor license application filed with the State of Arizona, to include brewing facilities, a kitchen, and seating. No opening date has been announced.

status: "tenants expected to start moving in in the coming months" / "An exact opening date has not been announced"
short_description: Oregano's, an Italian restaurant chain founded in Scottsdale in 1993, is opening a new location at Buckeye Commons West at the southwest corner of I-10 and Verrado Way in Buckeye. The shopping center's construction has completed, with tenants expected to start moving in in the coming months. No exact opening date has been announced.

Apothecary Cocktail Lounge, a pharmacy-themed craft cocktail bar from owner Eric Reid, is set to open at 3242 N. Clark Street in Chicago in May 2026, with a bar program led by Beverage Director Zachary Heller featuring signature cocktails organized into eight medicinal classifications. The concept blends the science of pharmacy with the artistry of craft cocktails, with a seasonal and Midwest hospitality-focused menu.

status: "locals can look forward to enjoying its cuisine before the year is out"
short_description: Coco Shrimp, a fast-casual shrimp-focused restaurant chain founded in 2016 with locations across DFW, is opening its 11th location at 2525 Wycliff Avenue, Suite 130 in Dallas's Oak Lawn area, per a filing with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation. The renovated 2,200 square-foot space is owned by partners Walter Cervin and Blaise Hadley, and an opening before year's end is anticipated. No exact opening date has been specified.

status: "Construction is currently wrapping up" / "an official opening date has not yet been announced"
short_description: Kyuramen, a Flushing, New York-based ramen chain with over 50 locations nationwide, is opening a location at 1 Wheeler Road in Burlington in a newly built structure at the corner of Middlesex Turnpike and Wheeler Road, confirmed by franchisee Charles Liu. The approximately 4,600 square-foot, 81-seat location is described as a more upscale "Kyuramen 3.0" concept, featuring party rooms, honeycomb booths, additional yakitori options, and an expanded drink menu. Construction is currently wrapping up; no official opening date has been announced.
